description: Offer command (DEX) — offer create and offer cancel with full flag support and on-chain state verification in E2E tests

userStories.title: Core offer create and offer cancel commands
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want to create and cancel DEX offers on XRPL so that I can exchange currencies without writing scripts.
acceptanceCriteria: New command file at src/commands/offer.ts with top-level 'offer' command and subcommands 'create' (alias 'c') and 'cancel' (alias 'x')
'offer create --taker-pays <amount> --taker-gets <amount> --seed <seed> --node testnet' submits OfferCreate and exits 0
Amount format: plain number = XRP drops conversion (e.g. '1.5' XRP), 'value/CURRENCY/issuerAddress' = IOU
On success, plain output prints: 'Offer created. Sequence: <N>'
'offer cancel --sequence <N> --seed <seed> --node testnet' submits OfferCancel and exits 0
'--json' flag: offer create outputs { hash, result, offerSequence }; offer cancel outputs { hash, result }
'--dry-run' flag: outputs serialized tx JSON ({ tx_blob, tx }) without submitting
'--no-wait' flag: submits without waiting for validation, exits 0 with 64-char hex hash in stdout
Key material options: --seed, --mnemonic, --account/--keystore/--password (consistent with trust.ts pattern)
Command registered in src/commands/index.ts and src/index.ts
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: OfferCreate flags: --sell, --passive, --immediate-or-cancel, --fill-or-kill, --expiration, --replace
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want full control over offer behavior using all standard OfferCreate flags.
acceptanceCriteria: '--sell' sets tfSell flag on the OfferCreate transaction
'--passive' sets tfPassive flag on the OfferCreate transaction
'--immediate-or-cancel' sets tfImmediateOrCancel flag
'--fill-or-kill' sets tfFillOrKill flag
'--expiration <iso-string>' sets the Expiration field; accepts ISO 8601 (e.g. '2030-01-01T00:00:00Z') and converts to XRPL epoch using xrpl.js isoTimeToRippleTime or equivalent
'--replace <sequence>' sets the OfferSequence field on OfferCreate (atomic cancel+replace)
Using '--immediate-or-cancel' and '--fill-or-kill' together exits 1 with stderr containing '--immediate-or-cancel and --fill-or-kill are mutually exclusive'
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: E2E tests: offer.validation.test.ts (no-network) and offer.core.test.ts (on-chain state verification)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E tests that verify actual ledger state after offer operations so we know the DEX integration is correct.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/e2e/offer/offer.validation.test.ts: no beforeAll, uses static dummy address rHb9CJAWyB4rj91VRWn96DkukG4bwdtyTh and seed snoPBrXtMeMyMHUVTgbuqAfg1SUTb; tests: missing --taker-pays exits 1, missing --taker-gets exits 1, missing key material exits 1, --immediate-or-cancel + --fill-or-kill exits 1 with mutual exclusion error
Create tests/e2e/offer/offer.core.test.ts: beforeAll funds 2 wallets via fundFromFaucet; sets up USD trust line between them using xrpl.js client directly

offer.core.test.ts test 'offer create XRP→IOU': run 'offer create --taker-pays 1/USD/<issuer> --taker-gets 10 --seed <seed> --node testnet'; query 'account offers --json <address>' and assert the new offer appears with matching taker_pays/taker_gets; also call client.request({ command: 'book_offers', taker_pays: { currency: 'XRP' }, taker_gets: { currency: 'USD', issuer: <issuer> } }) and assert the offer appears in the order book

offer.core.test.ts test 'offer cancel': pre-create an offer via xrpl.js directly in a beforeEach or inline setup; run 'offer cancel --sequence <seq> --seed <seed> --node testnet'; query 'account offers --json <address>' and assert the offer is no longer present
offer.core.test.ts test '--json output': assert stdout parses as JSON with hash (64-char hex), result 'tesSUCCESS', and offerSequence (number > 0)
offer.core.test.ts test '--dry-run': assert stdout parses as JSON with tx.TransactionType === 'OfferCreate' and tx_blob string; query account offers and assert count unchanged
offer.core.test.ts test '--no-wait': assert exit 0 and stdout matches /[0-9A-Fa-f]{64}/
offer.core.test.ts test '--sell flag': run offer create with --sell; verify offer appears in account_offers
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: E2E tests: offer.flags.test.ts (flag-specific on-chain verification)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want flag-specific E2E tests that verify on-chain effects of --passive, --replace, --expiration, and --immediate-or-cancel.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/e2e/offer/offer.flags.test.ts: beforeAll funds 2 wallets via fundFromFaucet and sets up USD trust line via xrpl.js
Test '--passive flag': run 'offer create --taker-pays 1/USD/<issuer> --taker-gets 10 --passive --seed <seed> --node testnet'; verify offer appears in 'account offers --json <address>'

Test '--replace flag': pre-create an offer via xrpl.js (record its sequence N); run 'offer create --taker-pays 1/USD/<issuer> --taker-gets 10 --replace <N> --seed <seed> --node testnet'; query account offers and assert original offer sequence N is gone AND a new offer is present
Test '--expiration flag': run offer create with '--expiration 2030-01-01T00:00:00Z'; query account offers and assert the offer entry has an 'expiration' field that is a positive number

Test '--immediate-or-cancel flag': run offer create with --immediate-or-cancel; assert exit 0 (command does not error); the offer may or may not remain in account_offers depending on ledger state — assert that if an offer remains it has the correct taker_pays/taker_gets, OR assert account_offers length has not increased (IOC offer consumed or cancelled)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
